description_long: This object consists of a group of four modern wireframe chairs, each featuring a geometric metal lattice structure for the seat and backrest. The chairs have a minimalist, mid-century design reminiscent of the Eames Wire Chair, with slender, angled metal legs connected by cross-bracing for stability. Each chair is equipped with a dark, rounded seat cushion that contrasts with the metallic wireframe, providing comfort while maintaining the open, airy aesthetic. The backrests are gently curved and rise to a rounded top, offering ergonomic support. The legs terminate in small, rounded feet, likely made of plastic or rubber to protect flooring. The overall appearance is sleek and contemporary, suitable for both urban outdoor and indoor environments.
description: A set of four modern wireframe chairs with dark seat cushions and slender metal legs, featuring a minimalist and airy design. The chairs have curved backrests and are visually striking with their geometric lattice structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wireframe structure, dark seat cushions, and the open, airy design of the chairs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved backrests, the thickness of the seat cushions, and the angled legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded tops of the backrests and the cross-bracing of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the wireframe construction and the profile of the seat and backrest.
